danger_signals.visual_form: Character model with grey gi transforming to purple gi at stage select
danger_signals.meaning: Presence of an enhanced, more dangerous opponent version; visual color shift signals power escalation and imminent threat

danger_signals.visual_form: Character portrait and dialogue box appearing mid-match sequence
danger_signals.meaning: Unexpected challenge from a character with personal vendetta; signals increased difficulty and narrative tension

safety_signals.visual_form: Fighter default costume colors (e.g., Akuma's grey gi in normal state)
safety_signals.meaning: Normal power state; indicates standard threat level for arcade mode progression

safety_signals.visual_form: Clear, uncorrupted portrait imagery on player select screen
safety_signals.meaning: Legitimate character state; corrupted portraits indicate anomalous/hacked versions with unpredictable behavior

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Rolento (member of Mad Gear, playable in Alpha 2, originated from Final Fight)
Evil Ryu (North American/EX version with darker fighting style)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Multiple character rivals indicate lineage-based faction system

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: M. Bison as selectable character and fixed final opponent for many character routes
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Final boss positioning in arcade mode hierarchy signals power structure

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Standard character rosters; eight-match sequences with fixed opponents
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Computer opponents follow predetermined patterns; predictable match sequences

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Rival character dialogue boxes interrupt match flow; character-specific rival encounters
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Rivals engage in pre-fight dialogue exchanges; opponent behavior shifts based on character relationship

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Shin Akuma appears with gi color transformation (grey to purple); enhanced visual model complexity
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Shin Akuma challenges before final boss, not as alternate final boss; indicates shifting threat hierarchy

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Final opponent identity varies by player character selection
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Each character has unique final matchup; narrative branching reflects character alliances/rivalries

environmental_storytelling.example: Inclusion of Sakura (schoolgirl inspired by Ryu's fighting), Gen (original Street Fighter assassin), and Rolento (Mad Gear member) creates narrative layers showing how Ryu's influence spreads and how disparate factions intersect
Each character has secret rival who interrupts matches; dialogue exchanges before combat convey personal history and factional tensions without exposition
Classic-style Chun-Li in Street Fighter II outfit; EX versions of characters with Street Fighter II movesets signal alternate timeline/faction representations
Grey gi becoming purple at stage select visualizes narrative progression toward dark version of protagonist; color shift communicates internal character state change

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Regular movesets and behavior patterns; uses standard Super Combo gauge mechanics
Appears as challenge before final boss; more powerful moveset; indicates player skill threshold; only accessible via specific unlock conditions
Interrupt normal match flow; initiate dialogue exchange; represent character-specific narrative branching; fight instead of scheduled opponent
Identity varies by player selection; fixed progression endpoint; signals completion of individual character's narrative arc

entity_behavioral_tells.entity: EX versions (Dhalsim, Zangief, Ryu, Ken, Chun-Li, Sagat, M. Bison)
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Use Street Fighter II movesets; lack access to Super Combo gauge; represent faction-specific fighting styles or alternate timeline versions

notes: Street Fighter Alpha 2 uses a character-centric narrative system where world-state and danger communication occurs through rival interruption mechanics, character roster composition, and costume/model variants. The Shin Akuma transformation (grey to purple gi) is a key visual signal for power escalation. Faction identity is communicated primarily through character selection (Mad Gear, Ryu's lineage, Shadaloo) rather than environmental design. Regional variants (EX characters, Evil Ryu) represent different faction representations or timeline branches. The game lacks explicit environmental danger/safety cues; threat communication is character-driven through opponent selection and dialogue systems.
sources_used: tcrf - Street Fighter Alpha 2 (SNES) - The Cutting Room Floor
